mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates month-over-month, signaling strengthening labor demand and consumer income expansion. This drives equity valuations upward through increased earnings expectations and reduced recession risk over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130996.0 thousand as of November 2009, recovering from 2008 trough
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with subsequent 20-day SPX returns (Spearman rank correlation < 0.15 over any 2-year rolling window), or positive payroll surprises do not produce positive forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero, maintaining an accommodative monetary stance that suppresses discount rates and sustains equity risk appetite. Continuation of this regime produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as long as inflation expectations remain anchored.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year change stays below 2.5%
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with stable inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of periods when both FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY < 2.5%, or Rank IC between the interaction term and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surge above 500 thousand units annualized, indicating renewed residential investment and construction employment. This demand impulse propagates through supply chains and consumer confidence, driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 550 thousand units in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 529.0 thousand as of October 2009, near recovery threshold
failure_condition: Monthly HOUST increases above 550 thousand fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or correlation between HOUST and subsequent 20-day returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines month-over-month, signaling demand weakness and excess capacity. This contraction raises recession risk and compresses equity valuations over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.4398 as of November 2009, still below pre-crisis peak
regime_hint: early recovery phase with fragile production momentum
failure_condition: Monthly INDPRO declines > 0.5% fail to precede negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or the predictive power of INDPRO momentum decays to Rank IC < 0.08 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerates while unemployment remains elevated, indicating consumer spending resilience despite labor slack. This demand strength, combined with monetary accommodation, produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.8% while UNRATE remains above 9.5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352073.0 million as of November 2009
regime_hint: jobless recovery with consumer spending resilience
failure_condition: The interaction signal (strong retail sales amid high unemployment) fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term drops below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, signaling accelerating labor market recovery. This improvement reduces recession risk and supports equity valuations upward over 60 trading days through both earnings growth and multiple expansion.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.3pp over any 3-month rolling window
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.0% as of November 2009, down from 10.0% peak in October
regime_hint: labor market recovery phase following cyclical trough
failure_condition: Three-month UNRATE declines > 0.3pp fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or the correlation between UNRATE momentum and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth accelerates while the federal funds rate remains at zero, creating a widening gap between economic growth and monetary accommodation. This regime produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate future rate normalization and multiple compression.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 3.0% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12990.3 billion as of Q3 2009
regime_hint: late-stage recovery with policy normalization risk
failure_condition: The interaction signal (strong GDP growth with zero rates) fails to predict negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales decline month-over-month while housing starts fall below 500 thousand units, indicating synchronized weakness in consumer and residential demand. This dual contraction signals recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity risk premiums widen.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls month-over-month and HOUST falls below 500 thousand units in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352073.0 million as of November 2009
fact:HOUST at 529.0 thousand as of October 2009
regime_hint: demand shock or early recession warning
failure_condition: Synchronized RSAFS and HOUST declines fail to precede negative 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window.
